  • Theoretical: IPCC Tier 2

Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology

Theoretical_basis.Rmd

Introduction

The herdr package performs environmental impact assessments based on the 2019 Refinement to the 2006 IPCC Guidelines. The core of the model is the Energy Balance, where Gross Energy (GE) intake is derived from the animal’s physiological requirements.

I. Ruminant Net Energy

1. Net Energy for Maintenance (NEmNE_m) (Eq. 10.3)

NEmNE_m is the energy required to maintain the animal’s energy balance without gain or loss of weight.

Formula: NEm=Cfi⋅Weight0.75NE_m = Cf_i \cdot Weight^{0.75}

Variable Description Source in the Package
NEmNE_m Net energy for maintenance (MJ/day) Calculated
CfiCf_i Maintenance coefficient based on animal species/status ipcc_coefficients.csv
WeightWeight Live weight of the individual animal (kg) weights.csv

2. Net Energy for Activity (NEaNE_a)

NEaNE_a represents energy used for grazing, walking, or other activities.

  • Cattle: NEa=Ca⋅NEmNE_a = C_a \cdot NE_m (Eq. 10.4)
  • Sheep and Goats: NEa=Ca⋅WeightNE_a = C_a \cdot Weight (Eq. 10.5)
Variable Description Source in the Package
NEaNE_a Net energy for activity (MJ/day) Calculated
CaC_a Activity coefficient (varies by feeding situation) ipcc_coefficients.csv
NEmNE_m Maintenance energy Result of Eq. 10.3
WeightWeight Live weight of the animal (kg) weights.csv

3. Net Energy for Growth (NEgNE_g)

Energy required for tissue deposition (weight gain).

  • Cattle and Buffalo: (Eq. 10.6) NEg=22.02⋅(BWC⋅MW)0.75⋅WG1.097NE_{g} = 22.02 \cdot \left( \frac{BW}{C \cdot MW} \right)^{0.75} \cdot WG^{1.097}

  • Sheep and Goats: (Eq. 10.7) NEg=WGlamb/kid⋅(a+0.5b(BWi+BWf))365NE_g = \frac{WG_{lamb/kid} \cdot (a + 0.5b(BW_i + BW_f))}{365}

Variable Description Source in the Package
BWBW Average live body weight of the animal (kg) weights.csv
MWMW Mature body weight of an adult (kg) weights.csv
WGWG Daily weight gain (kg/day) weights.csv
C,a,bC, a, b IPCC species-specific growth constants ipcc_coefficients.csv
BWi/BWfBW_i / BW_f Initial and final weight for the period (kg) weights.csv

4. Net Energy for Lactation (NElNE_l)

Energy required for milk production based on volume and fat content.

  • Cattle: NEl=Milk⋅(1.47+0.40⋅Fat)NE_l = Milk \cdot (1.47 + 0.40 \cdot Fat) (Eq. 10.8)
  • Sheep and Goats: NEl=Milk⋅EVmilkNE_l = Milk \cdot EV_{milk} (Eq. 10.9)
Variable Description Source in the Package
NElNE_l Net energy for lactation (MJ/day) Calculated
MilkMilk Average daily milk yield (kg/day) livestock_definitions.csv
FatFat Milk fat content percentage (%) livestock_definitions.csv
EVmilkEV_{milk} Energy required to produce 1 kg of milk (MJ/kg) ipcc_coefficients.csv

5. Net Energy for Work, Wool, and Pregnancy

  • Work (NEworkNE_{work}): NEwork=0.10⋅NEm⋅HoursNE_{work} = 0.10 \cdot NE_m \cdot Hours
  • Wool (NEwoolNE_{wool}): NEwool=Prwool⋅EVwool365NE_{wool} = \frac{Pr_{wool} \cdot EV_{wool}}{365}
  • Pregnancy (NEpNE_p): NEp=Cpregnancy⋅NEmNE_p = C_{pregnancy} \cdot NE_m
Variable Description Source in the Package
HoursHours Number of hours worked per day (draft animals) livestock_definitions.csv
PrwoolPr_{wool} Annual wool production (kg/year) livestock_definitions.csv
EVwoolEV_{wool} Energy per kg of wool (IPCC default 24 MJ/kg) Constant
CpregnancyC_{pregnancy} Pregnancy energy coefficient ipcc_coefficients.csv

6. Efficiency Ratios (REMREM and REGREG)

Based on IPCC Equations 10.14 and 10.15, derived from feed digestibility (DEDE):

  • For Maintenance (REMREM): REM=[1.123−(4.092⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.126⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(25.4DE)]REM = \left[ 1.123 - (4.092 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.126 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{25.4}{DE} \right) \right]
  • For Growth (REGREG): REG=[1.164−(5.16⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.308⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(37.4DE)]REG = \left[ 1.164 - (5.16 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.308 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{37.4}{DE} \right) \right]
Variable Description Source in the Package
REMREM Efficiency of digestible energy for maintenance Calculated
REGREG Efficiency of digestible energy for growth Calculated
DEDE Digestibility of feed as % of gross energy feed_characteristics.csv

7. Gross Energy (GE)

Gross Energy (GE) is the total daily energy intake required to sustain all physiological functions.

Formula: GE=(NEm+NEa+NEl+NEwork+NEpREM+NEg+NEwoolREG)DE/100GE = \frac{\left( \frac{NE_m + NE_a + NE_l + NE_{work} + NE_p}{REM} + \frac{NE_g + NE_{wool}}{REG} \right)}{DE/100}

Variable Description Source in the Package
GEGE Total daily gross energy requirement (MJ/day) Calculated
NExNE_x Various net energy requirements calculated above Calculated Results
REM/REGREM/REG Efficiency ratios Results of Eq. 10.14/10.15
DEDE Feed digestibility (%) feed_characteristics.csv

II. Monogastric Metabolizable Energy (ME)

For swine and poultry, energy requirements are estimated directly as metabolizable energy (ME) following FEDNA recommendations, rather than through the IPCC net energy system used for ruminants.

1. Maintenance

Maintenance requirements are calculated as:

MEmaint=Cfi×WαME_{maint} = C_{fi} \times W^{\alpha}

Variable Description Source in Package
MEmaintME_{maint} Daily metabolizable energy requirement (kcal/day) Calculated
CfiC_{fi} Maintenance coefficient monogastric_definitions.csv
WW Mean live weight (kg) livestock_weights.csv
α\alpha Species-specific metabolic exponent. monogastric_definitions.csv

Note: Poultry has a fixed alpha of 0.75.

2. Growth

Poultry

MEgrowth=13.4×Fat×WG+12.0×Protein×WGME_{growth} = 13.4 \times Fat \times WG + 12.0 \times Protein \times WG

Swine

MEgrowth=12.8×Fat×WG+12.1×Protein×WGME_{growth} = 12.8 \times Fat \times WG + 12.1 \times Protein \times WG

Variable Description Source in Package
WGWG Average daily weight gain (g/day) Calculated
FatFat Fraction of body fat deposited per kg of body weight gain (kg fat/kg gain) monogastric_definitions.csv
ProteinProtein Fraction of body protein deposited per kg of body weight gain (kg protein/kg gain) monogastric_definitions.csv

3. Egg Production (Poultry)

For laying hens:

MEeggs=2×EggmassME_{eggs} = 2 \times Egg_{mass}

Where EggmassEgg_{mass} is the daily egg mass produced (g/day).

4. Gestation (Swine)

Conceptus development:

MEconceptus=2600×BWbirth×PigletsDaysgest+55.175ME_{conceptus} = \frac{2600 \times BW_{birth} \times Piglets}{Days_{gest}} + 55.175

Variable Description Source in Package
DaysgestDays_{gest} Period of gestation (day) livestock_weights.csv
BWbirthBW_{birth} Body Weight at birth (kg) livestock_weights.csv
PigletsPiglets Number of born piglets monogastric_definitions.csv

Note: The constant 55.175 represents the mammary gland weight gain in kcal/day.

Metabolizable energy for the reserve gain:

MEreserves=4800×ReservegainDaysgestME_{reserves} = \frac{4800 \times Reserve_{gain}}{Days_{gest}}

Variable Description Source in Package
ReservegainReserve_{gain} Body reserve gain during gestation (kg) livestock_weights.csv
DaysgestDays_{gest} Gestation length (days) livestock_weights.csv

Total gestation requirement:

MEgestation=MEconceptus+MEreservesME_{gestation} = ME_{conceptus} + ME_{reserves}

5. Lactation (Swine)

Milk production:

MEmilk=(6.83×WGpiglet−125)×PigletssucklingME_{milk} = (6.83 \times WG_{piglet} - 125) \times Piglets_{suckling}

Variable Description Source in Package
WGpigletWG_{piglet} Average daily weight gain of a suckling piglet (g/day) Calculated
PigletssucklingPiglets_{suckling} Number of suckling piglets per sow monogastric_definitions.csv

Mobilization of body reserves:

MEmobilization=−28183DayslactationME_{mobilization} = -\frac{28183}{Days_{lactation}}

Variable Description Source in Package
DayslactationDays_{lactation} Lactation length (days) livestock_weights.csv

Total lactation requirement:

MElactation=MEmilk+MEmobilizationME_{lactation} = ME_{milk} + ME_{mobilization}

6. Total Metabolizable Energy

The total daily metabolizable energy requirement is:

MEtotal=MEmaint+MEgrowth+MEeggs+MEgestation+MElactationME_{total} = ME_{maint} + ME_{growth} + ME_{eggs} + ME_{gestation} + ME_{lactation}

III. Methane Module

1. Enteric Fermentation (CH4CH_4)

Methane Emission Factor (EFEF) (Eq. 10.21)

EF=GE⋅(Ym100)⋅36555.65EF = \frac{GE \cdot \left( \frac{Y_m}{100} \right) \cdot 365}{55.65}

Variable Description Source in Package
EFEF Methane emission factor (kg CH4CH_4/head/year) Calculated
GEGE Daily gross energy intake (MJ/day) Result of Section I
YmY_m Methane conversion factor (%) feed_characteristics.csv

Total Enteric Emissions (ETE_T) (Eq. 10.19)

ET=∑(P)EF(T,P)⋅(N(T,P)106)E_T = \sum_{(P)} EF_{(T,P)} \cdot \left( \frac{N_{(T,P)}}{10^6} \right)

Variable Description Source in Package
ETE_T Total emissions from category TT (Gg CH4CH_4/year) Calculated
N(T,P)N_{(T,P)} Number of animals in category TT livestock_census.csv

2. Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)})

Volatile Solid Excretion Rates (VSVS)

Ruminants (IPCC 2019, Eq. 10.24)

VS=[GE⋅(1−DE100)+(UE⋅GE)]⋅[1−ASH18.45]VS = \left[ GE \cdot \left(1-\frac{DE}{100}\right) + (UE \cdot GE) \right] \cdot \left[ \frac{1-ASH}{18.45} \right]

Poultry (FEDNA-based)

VS=DMI⋅(1−MEfeedGEfeed)⋅(1−ASH)VS = DMI \cdot \left( 1-\frac{ME_{feed}}{GE_{feed}} \right) \cdot (1-ASH)

Swine (FEDNA-based)

VS=DMI⋅(1−DEfeedGEfeed+0.02)⋅(1−ASH)VS = DMI \cdot \left( 1-\frac{DE_{feed}}{GE_{feed}}+0.02 \right) \cdot (1-ASH)

Variable Description Source in Package
VSVS Daily volatile solids excretion (kg VS/day) Calculated
GEGE Gross energy intake (MJ/day) Calculated
DEDE Digestible energy of the diet (%) feed_characteristics.csv
UEUE Urinary energy fraction (default = 0.04) Function argument (urinary_energy)
DMIDMI Dry matter intake (kg/day) Calculated
MEfeedME_{feed} Metabolizable energy of poultry diets (kcal/kg DM) feed_characteristics.csv
DEfeedDE_{feed} Digestible energy of swine diets (kcal/kg DM) feed_characteristics.csv
GEfeedGE_{feed} Gross energy of the feed (kcal/kg DM) feed_characteristics.csv
ASHASH Ash content of the diet (fraction of DM) feed_characteristics.csv

Manure Emission Factor (EFEF) (Eq. 10.23)

EF(T)=(VS(T)⋅365)⋅[B0(T)⋅0.67⋅∑S,kMCFS,k100⋅AWMS(T,S,k)]EF_{(T)} = (VS_{(T)} \cdot 365) \cdot \left[ B_{0(T)} \cdot 0.67 \cdot \sum_{S,k} \frac{MCF_{S,k}}{100} \cdot AWMS_{(T,S,k)} \right]

Variable Description Source in Package
B0(T)B_{0(T)} Maximum methane producing capacity (m3m^3/kg VS) ipcc_coefficients.csv
MCFS,kMCF_{S,k} Methane conversion factor for system SS (%) ipcc_mm.csv
AWMSAWMS Fraction of manure handled in system SS manure_management.csv

IV. Nitrous Oxide Module

1. Direct Emissions

1.1 Nitrogen Intake (NintakeN_{intake})

Nitrogen intake is calculated differently for ruminants and monogastric animals.

For ruminants, nitrogen intake is estimated from gross energy intake and dietary crude protein (IPCC Eq. 10.31/10.32):

Nintake=(GE18.45)⋅(CP/1006.25)N_{intake} = \left( \frac{GE}{18.45} \right) \cdot \left( \frac{CP/100}{6.25} \right)

For swine and poultry, nitrogen intake is calculated directly from dry matter intake:

Nintake=DMI⋅(CP/1006.25)N_{intake} = DMI \cdot \left( \frac{CP/100}{6.25} \right)

Variable Description Source in Package
NintakeN_{intake} Daily nitrogen intake (kg N/head/day) Calculated
GEGE Gross energy intake (MJ/day) Calculated
DMIDMI Dry matter intake (kg/day) Calculated
CPCP Crude protein concentration of the diet (%) feed_characteristics.csv

1.2 Nitrogen Retention (NretentionN_{retention})

Nitrogen retention represents the fraction of nitrogen retained in animal products (milk, body tissues, eggs or offspring) rather than being excreted.

Cattle

Milk protein concentration is estimated as:

Milkprotein=1.9+0.4×FatcontentMilk_{protein} = 1.9 + 0.4 \times Fat_{content}

For lactating and growing animals:

Nretention=Milk×(Milkprotein/100)6.38+WG(268−7.03×NEgWG)/10006.25N_{retention} = \frac{Milk \times (Milk_{protein}/100)}{6.38} + \frac{WG \left(268-\frac{7.03 \times NE_g}{WG}\right)/1000}{6.25}

Variable Description Source in Package
NretentionN_{retention} Daily nitrogen retained (kg N/head/day) Calculated
MilkMilk Daily milk yield (kg/day) livestock_definitions.csv
MilkproteinMilk_{protein} Estimated milk protein concentration (%) Calculated
FatcontentFat_{content} Milk fat content (%) livestock_definitions.csv
WGWG Daily body weight gain (kg/day) Calculated
NEgNE_g Net energy for growth (MJ/day) Calculated

For mature lactating animals, only the milk term is considered. For growing animals, only the growth term is considered.

Sheep and Goats

Following the IPCC Tier 2 methodology, nitrogen retention is assumed to be a constant value:

Nretention=0.1N_{retention} = 0.1

Poultry

For growing birds:

Nretention=(BWfinal−BWinitial)×0.028DaysN_{retention} = \frac{(BW_{final} - BW_{initial}) \times 0.028}{Days}

Variable Description Source in Package
BWfinalBW_{final} Final body weight (kg) livestock_weights.csv
BWinitialBW_{initial} Initial body weight (kg) livestock_weights.csv
DaysDays Productive period (days) livestock_weights.csv

For laying hens:

Nretention=0.028×WG+0.0185×Eggmass1000N_{retention} = 0.028 \times WG + \frac{0.0185 \times Egg_{mass}}{1000}

Variable Description Source in Package
WGWG Daily body weight gain (kg/day) Calculated
EggmassEgg_{mass} Daily egg mass production (g/day) monogastric_definitions.csv
Swine

For growing pigs:

Nretention=(BWfinal−BWinitial)×NgainDaysN_{retention} = \frac{(BW_{final} - BW_{initial}) \times N_{gain}}{Days}

Variable Description Source in Package
BWfinalBW_{final} Final body weight (kg) livestock_weights.csv
BWinitialBW_{initial} Initial body weight (kg) livestock_weights.csv
DaysDays Productive period (days) livestock_weights.csv
NgainN_{gain} Nitrogen retention coefficient according to final body weight IPCC (2019)

For breeding sows:

Nretention=Ngain,sow+Nweaned365N_{retention} = \frac{N_{gain,sow} + N_{weaned}}{365}

with

Ngain,sow=0.025×FR×SkgN_{gain,sow} = 0.025 \times FR \times S_{kg}

and

Nweaned=0.025×Piglets×FR×(Wweaning−Wbirth)0.98N_{weaned} = \frac{0.025 \times Piglets \times FR \times (W_{weaning} - W_{birth})}{0.98}

Variable Description Source in Package
Ngain,sowN_{gain,sow} Annual nitrogen retained in sow body tissues (kg N/year) Calculated
NweanedN_{weaned} Annual nitrogen retained in weaned piglets (kg N/year) Calculated
FRFR Annual farrowing rate (farrowings/year) Calculated
SkgS_{kg} Sow body weight change between consecutive parturitions (kg/year) Calculated
PigletsPiglets Number of piglets born per litter monogastric_definitions.csv
WweaningW_{weaning} Piglet weaning weight (kg) livestock_weights.csv
WbirthW_{birth} Piglet birth weight (kg) livestock_weights.csv

1.3 Nitrogen Excretion (NexcretedN_{excreted})

For cattle, swine and poultry, annual nitrogen excretion is calculated as:

Nexcreted=(Nintake−Nretention)×365N_{excreted} = (N_{intake} - N_{retention}) \times 365

For sheep and goats, the IPCC assumes a fixed nitrogen retention fraction of 10%:

Nexcreted=Nintake×(1−0.1)×365N_{excreted} = N_{intake} \times (1 - 0.1) \times 365

Variable Description Source in Package
NexcretedN_{excreted} Annual nitrogen excreted (kg N/head/year) Calculated
NintakeN_{intake} Daily nitrogen intake (kg N/head/day) Calculated
NretentionN_{retention} Daily nitrogen retained (kg N/head/day) Calculated

1.4 Direct N2ON_2O Emissions

Direct nitrous oxide emissions from manure management are calculated following IPCC (2019, Eq. 10.25):

N2Odirect=Population×Nexcreted×Allocation×EF3×4428N_2O_{direct} = Population \times N_{excreted} \times Allocation \times EF_3 \times \frac{44}{28}

Variable Description Source in Package
PopulationPopulation Number of animals livestock_census.csv
NexcretedN_{excreted} Annual nitrogen excreted (kg N/head/year) Calculated
AllocationAllocation Fraction of manure assigned to a management system manure_management.csv
EF3EF_3 Direct emission factor for the manure management system ipcc_mm.csv

2. Indirect Emissions

2.1 Nitrogen Losses from Manure

Volatilization (Nvolatilization−MMSN_{volatilization-MMS}) (Eq. 10.26) Nvol−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracGasMS]N_{vol-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot N_{ex} \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{GasMS} \right]

Leaching and Runoff (Nleaching−MMSN_{leaching-MMS}) (Eq. 10.27) Nleach−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracLeachMS]N_{leach-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot N_{ex} \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{LeachMS} \right]

Variable Description Source in Package
FracGasMSFrac_{GasMS} Fraction of N lost as volatilized gas (NH3+NOxNH_3 + NO_x) ipcc_mm.csv
FracLeachMSFrac_{LeachMS} Fraction of N lost via leaching into water ipcc_mm.csv

2.2 Indirect N2ON_2O Calculation (Eq. 10.28/10.29)

  • From Volatilization: N2OG(mm)=(Nvol−MMS⋅EF4)⋅4428N_2O_{G(mm)} = (N_{vol-MMS} \cdot EF_4) \cdot \frac{44}{28}
  • From Leaching: N2OL(mm)=(Nleach−MMS⋅EF5)⋅4428N_2O_{L(mm)} = (N_{leach-MMS} \cdot EF_5) \cdot \frac{44}{28}
Variable Description Source in Package
EF4EF_4 Indirect N2ON_2O factor for volatilization ipcc_mm.csv
EF5EF_5 Indirect N2ON_2O factor for leaching ipcc_mm.csv

📚 References

  • IPCC (2019). Refinement to the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories. Volume 4: AFOLU, Chapter 10.

On this page

Developed by Juan Carlos Baez, Jon Sampedro, Guillermo Pardo, Agustín del Prado.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
